expected_behavior: Must read collectors-matrix.md then collectors-orm.md. Approach A (has @nestjs/config). Offer nest-profiler-mikro-orm and flag the ESM-only gotcha: it is the only ESM-only collector, imported AFTER MikroOrmModule; register MikroOrmCollectorModule.forRoot({ slowThreshold: 100 }) (slowThreshold, NOT slowQueryThreshold) — no dataSource needed. Should offer the MikroOrmSchemaCollectorModule entity panel too.

query:
package.json includes @nestjs/config, @nestjs/axios and axios.

User: I want to see the outgoing HTTP calls my app makes.
expected_behavior: Must read collectors-http.md. Offer nest-profiler-http and apply the central gotcha: NOTHING is captured unless an instrumentation is listed — HttpCollectorModule.forRoot({ instrumentations: [AxiosInstrumentation] }) with AxiosInstrumentation imported from '@eleven-labs/nest-profiler-http/axios', and HttpModule imported in the SAME module. Must NOT claim capture is automatic with an empty instrumentations array.

query:
package.json includes @nestjs/config, class-validator and class-transformer. main.ts already contains app.useGlobalPipes(new ValidationPipe({ whitelist: true })).

User: Add DTO validation profiling.
expected_behavior: Must read collectors-validator.md. Offer nest-profiler-validator app-owned: keep the pipe in main.ts but wrap it — app.useGlobalPipes(createProfilerValidationPipe(createClassValidatorPipe({ whitelist: true, transform: true }))) (createClassValidatorPipe, not a bare ValidationPipe, so per-property violations reach the panel), and register the panel with ValidatorCollectorModule.forRoot() gated like the core. Only one global validation pipe. Note DTOs need value imports (not 'import type').

query:
package.json includes @nestjs/config and nest-commander. The app has both an HTTP server (main.ts) and a CLI entrypoint (cli.ts using CommandFactory.run).

User: I want to profile my CLI commands and see them in the profiler UI.
expected_behavior: Must read collectors-simple.md. Offer nest-profiler-commander and apply the cross-process gotcha: CLI and HTTP are separate processes, so set storageType: 'file' (or sqlite) on the core; register CommanderCollectorModule.forRoot() in the module bootstrapped by CommandFactory.run AND import it in the HTTP app. Recommend adding .profiler/ to .gitignore.

query:
package.json includes @nestjs/config, @nestjs/graphql, @nestjs/apollo and graphql.

User: Add GraphQL profiling.
expected_behavior: Must read collectors-simple.md. Offer nest-profiler-graphql with GraphQLCollectorModule.forRoot() (the module is GraphQLCollectorModule, NOT ProfilerGraphQLModule) alongside GraphQLModule. Apply the gotcha: the GraphQLModule context MUST expose the request — for Apollo, context: ({ req }) => ({ req }). May suggest composing the core ignoreRequest with combineFilters(ignoreGraphQLPlayground, ignoreGraphQLIntrospection).

query: Does the TypeORM collector need inject: [DataSource]?
expected_behavior: Must read collectors-orm.md. Answer: NO. TypeOrmCollectorModule self-resolves the DataSource via getDataSourceToken(connectionName), so forRoot() works with no inject. Use forRootAsync only to drive options (e.g. slowThreshold) from ConfigService, and pass connectionName for a non-default DataSource. Place it after TypeOrmModule.

query: Should the config and validator collectors go in the root module or a feature module?
expected_behavior: Must read collectors-matrix.md. Answer: at the root — they contribute global panels (a bootstrap snapshot for config; validator's panel, with its pipe app-owned in main.ts); routes and commander are root-level too. Infra-scoped collectors (database, http, cache, rabbitmq, graphql transport) stay co-located in the feature module that owns their infrastructure.
